name: BROKEN TOP BOTTLE SHOP & ALE CAFE

fr.elements: Main Building; Roof System; Vehicle Parking Area; Pedestrian Walkways; Landscaped Area
fr.considerations: Single Access Point; Limited Side Access
fr.recommendations: Improving emergency response effectiveness on this property could benefit from several strategic enhancements. Establishing alternative access routes or expanding the existing single access point might facilitate quicker entry and egress for emergency vehicles. Additionally, maintaining clear and unobstructed pedestrian walkways can support responder movement and potentially expedite the transport of equipment and personnel. Creating additional side access paths or clearing pathways along the periphery of the property could assist in increasing visibility and maneuverability for emergency responders. Ensuring that the vehicle parking area is efficiently organized and avoids any encroachment on critical movement corridors could further enhance the overall staging capabilities and operational flexibility during emergency situations.

wildfire.conditions: Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Defensible Space; Scattered Trees; Vegetation Adjacent to Access Route; Vegetated Perimeter; Scattered Shrubs; Tree Cluster; Limited Rear Access
wildfire.recommendations: Creating greater separation between vegetation and structures and managing the vegetative perimeter could enhance defensible space and reduce fuel continuity around the property. Attention to scattered shrubs and tree clusters could involve thinning or selective removal to minimize potential fire spread and support long-term vegetation management practices. Maintaining the access route by reducing vegetation congestion can improve firefighter access and suppression support. Enhancing rear access through strategic vegetation management could further facilitate emergency response. Overall, these actions may improve the property's resilience to wildfires by reducing excessive vegetation concentrations around structures and access points.
